findings.evidence: `FiniteTypeProduct.lean:74-109` defines `pointsMulEquiv` and only `pointsMulEquiv_fst`, `pointsMulEquiv_snd`, `pointsMulEquiv_symm_apply`; the generic API has `AffineGroup.Product.pointsMulEquiv_mapValue` at `Product.lean:168` and inverse naturality at `Product.lean:197`, and analogous point-equivalence files expose `pointsMulEquiv_mapValue` (`Trivial.lean:119`, `SplitTorus.lean:106`).
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` finite-type versions of `pointsMulEquiv_mapValue`, the fst/snd forms, and `mapValue_pointsMulEquiv_symm_apply` (with the pure-tensor evaluation forms if intended for pointwise use), delegating to `AffineGroup.Product.*`.
findings.issue: `FiniteTypeCommHopfAlgCat.pointsMulEquiv` is exposed with projection and inverse formulas only; it lacks the `mapValue` naturality lemmas that the underlying product equivalence and other points equivalences provide.

summary: The finite-type product wrapper exposes the product-points equivalence but omits the value-algebra naturality API that characterizes this functor-of-points equivalence. Add finite-type wrappers for the existing generic naturality lemmas so downstream users can work through the finite-type interface without unfolding the opaque definition.
